subject: Re: WARP 1.1 Particle Extraction Issue
body:
Hi All,

Any updates on WARP 1.1 development? I'd like to collect in real-time on 
the A6000 GPUs, but thats not possible with the WARP 1.1 extraction issue 
(A6000 GPUs are not compatible with WARP 0.9). I'm currently extracting in 
CS, but the workflow is slower and inconvenient. 

Thanks,
Matt

On Wednesday, November 16, 2022 at 11:02:42 AM UTC-5 mjja...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hi Dimitry,
>
> Update, I imported the WARP 1.1 goodparticle**.star particles and 
> micrographs (from average folder) into Cryosparc, connected them, and 
> extracted the particles in Cryosparc. The 2D class looks fine (same as WARP 
> 1.0.9 2D class above), so there is a bug with the WARP 1.1 beta particle 
> extraction. 
>
> Hope this is an easy fix.
>
> Best,
> Matt
>
> On Wednesday, November 16, 2022 at 9:30:53 AM UTC-5 mjja...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Can anyone else confirm that they do or don't have this issue in WARP 1.1?
>>
>> Thank you,
>> Matt
>>
>> On Friday, November 11, 2022 at 1:18:38 PM UTC-5 mjja...@gmail.com wrote:
>>
>>> Hello,
>>>
>>> I’m having issues with particle extraction in WARP 1.1. BoxNet seems to 
>>> pick the particle locations correctly (image1). But when imported into 
>>> cryospcarc there are issues:
>>>
>>> Import job (image2)
>>> 2D class rd 2 (image3)
>>> 2D class rd 3 (image4) (2D class then takes about 10 iterations to get 
>>> back to rd 2)
>>> 2D class rd 20 (image5) (artifact is the most prominent class)
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> I’ve tested with the same dataset on WARP 0.9 and this artifact does not 
>>> show up (image 6+7).
>>>
>>>
>>> Dimitry, do you see this issue with your workstation?
>>>
>>>  
>>>
>>> Thank you,
>>> Matt
>>>
>>>>
